rules_evaluated.evidence: line:49: `m4/<short-task-slug>` — angle-bracket placeholder inside fenced prompt block the student copies
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace <short-task-slug> with a back-reference: 'named m4/<use the task slug we agreed on>' or have Claude derive it from scrollback context ('name the branch after the task we scoped, prefixed m4/').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in — Prompt 3 (optional nudge).
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:69: 'If Claude stalls and you want to see if it picks itself back up, dark-humour nudge — phrased as encouragement, lands as a taunt:' — descriptive framing, not a command-verb lead-in under 20 words.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace with a canonical lead-in, e.g. 'Nudge Claude back into motion with a single one-liner.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
No bold markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks.
Minimal hand-off + Claude self-augments for send-off prompts.
Sessions are the unit of work.
Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Known correct Claude Code facts — session transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/.
Source URL + type label — mandatory for every practitioner reference.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:89: 'Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen, You're the Bread in the AI Sandwich' — URL present, attribution correct, but no [practitioner analysis] source-type label. line:91: 'Kieran Klaassen, Compound Engineering' — URL present, no [practitioner direct] label.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add bracket source-type labels: [practitioner analysis] for the Entis piece, [practitioner direct] for Klaassen's own article. These are pre-reads, but they carry practitioner references and the rule fires on those.
